title: Their fight made my ease possible
subtitle: An affirmation for receiving abundance without guilt when the women before you didn't have it.
excerpt: A short affirmation for releasing the guilt of having more than the women in your family. The statement names the truth: your ease doesn't erase their struggle. It is built on it.
sourceNotes: Adapted from Day 12 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025), "Forgive ancestors' financial pain."

practice.whenToUse: Use when guilt about thriving arrives, especially when you notice you are pulling back from abundance to feel closer to the women in your line.

content.text: An affirmation for when thriving feels like betrayal. The statement holds two things at once: their difficulty was real, and your ease is the continuation of what they made possible.
Their fight made my ease possible. I honour them by receiving it. My abundance is the continuation of their effort.

Say it out loud, hand on heart, three times in the morning. On days when guilt is loud, sit with the middle line: "I honour them by receiving it." That is the reframe. Pulling back from ease is not how you honour them.

Adapted from Day 12 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025), which addresses the ancestral money grief that can show up as guilt around having more than the previous generation had.
